title: It seems/appears (that) + S + V

formulas.zh: 看起來似乎……（用 that 子句描述推測內容）

formulas.formula: It seems/appears as if/as though + S + V ...
formulas.zh: 看起來好像……（語氣更強調「彷彿」的感覺）

explanation: 此句型以虛主詞 It 開頭，搭配 seems 或 appears 表示「看起來、似乎」，用於根據外在觀察或間接證據做出推測，語氣較委婉、不確定。that 可省略，後接完整子句。若改用 as if / as though，則強調「彷彿、好像」的假設語氣。seem 較口語常用，appear 較正式書面。注意：主詞也可改為人或事物，如 He seems to be tired，但本句型聚焦於 It 開頭的用法。

examples.eng: It seems that the weather will get worse this weekend.
examples.chi: 看起來這個週末天氣會變得更糟。

examples.eng: It appears that the new policy has had a positive effect on students.
examples.chi: 這項新政策似乎對學生產生了正面的影響。

examples.eng: It seems as if nobody cares about the environmental problems around us.
examples.chi: 似乎沒有人關心我們周遭的環境問題。

examples.eng: It appears that she has already made up her mind to study abroad.
examples.chi: 她似乎已經下定決心要出國留學了。

extension.tip: seem 與 appear 皆表推測，但 appear 較正式，常見於新聞或學術寫作。若要表達「過去看起來如何」，可用 It seemed/appeared that...。另外，as if 後面若描述與事實相反的情境，動詞常用過去式（假設語氣）。

examples.test: It seems that he is honest. vs. He seems to be honest.
examples.result: → 兩句意思相同，前者用 that 子句，後者用不定詞，皆表示「他似乎很誠實」。

examples.test: It appears as if she knew the answer. (但她其實不知道)
examples.result: → as if 後用過去式 knew，表示與事實相反的假設語氣，意為「她看起來好像知道答案（但其實不知道）」。

examples.test: It seems that it will rain. vs. It looks like it will rain.
examples.result: → seems that 較正式委婉，looks like 較口語直接，兩者皆可表達「看起來要下雨了」。

extension.note: seem 推測較主觀，appear 推測較客觀；as if 後面接假設，that 後面接事實。

parts: the students are not satisfied with the exam results.
exercises.sentence: It seems that the students are not satisfied with the exam results.
exercises.translation: 學生們似乎對考試結果不滿意。

parts: that the company will close down by the end of this year.
exercises.sentence: It appears that the company will close down by the end of this year.
exercises.translation: 這家公司似乎會在今年年底前關閉。

parts: he had never heard of the news before.
exercises.sentence: It seems as if he had never heard of the news before.
exercises.translation: 他看起來好像從來沒聽過這個消息。

parts: technology has completely changed the way we communicate.
exercises.sentence: It appears that technology has completely changed the way we communicate.
exercises.translation: 科技似乎已經徹底改變了我們溝通的方式。
